basics.name: Shuting Du
basics.label: PhD Candidate in Electrical and Computer Engineering
basics.summary: PhD candidate specializing in high-performance, low-power AI systems and hardware-software co-design. Proven expertise in developing 3D-IC architectures, compute-in-memory chips, and heterogeneous SoC accelerators. Skilled in modeling, designing, and optimizing AI/ML workloads across device, circuit, architecture, and system levels.

work.name: NanoX Lab, Purdue University
highlights: Designed 3D-CIMlet, a heterogeneous chiplet framework for in-memory acceleration of edge LLM inference and continual learning, with thermal-aware modeling and multi-die design exploration for 2.5D/3D systems.
Developed memory-reliability-aware chiplet mapping strategies for heterogeneous integration of RRAM, eDRAM, and hybrid dies across mixed technology nodes.
Developed floating-point compute-in-memory engines with RRAM-eDRAM fusion macros, 3D-MAC dataflow, pipelined accumulation, and power-aware synthesis.
Built cross-layer frameworks for vector-symbolic computing and system-technology co-optimization across DNN, LLM, VSA, and optimization workloads.

work.name: Center of Computational Evolutionary Intelligence, Duke University
work.summary: Advised by Prof. Yiran Chen and Prof. Helen (Hai) Li.
highlights: Designed an 8T SRAM-CIM macro supporting multi-bit MAC operations for edge AI and up to 3-bit sensing read-out circuits.
Implemented 8T computing cells and multi-bit current-domain read-out circuits with a customized flash ADC to improve area efficiency.
Developed an auto error correction scheme with digital near-memory processing.

work.name: Efficient, Secure and Intelligent Computing Lab, Arizona State University
highlights: Developed the PIMA-SIM processing-in-memory evaluation tool on top of NVSIM to extract delay, energy, and area data for non-volatile memory and SRAM PIM technologies.
Improved PIM-level configuration flexibility with customized row activation schemes and sub-array, mat, and bank-level components.
Evaluated high-density 28 nm STT-assisted SOT-MRAM, showing 95.4% lower energy, 1.6x speedup, and 65% lower area overhead compared with SRAM.

work.name: SEU-FEI Nano-Pico Center, Southeast University
highlights: Built EOG signal collection, 300x amplification, and 50 Hz low-pass filtering modules using INA333, OPA2333, and right-leg-drive circuitry.
Designed and tested the PCB in Altium Designer and analyzed signal-processing performance in Matlab with the SEED database.

interests.name: Research Interests
keywords: Cross-layer algorithm-hardware co-design for AI acceleration
Energy-efficient and scalable 3D heterogeneous System-on-Chip architectures
Memory-centric hardware accelerators based on emerging technologies

projects.name: Programmable Accelerator Architecture
projects.summary: GPU kernel and scheduler optimization with C++ and CUDA.
highlights: Developed CUDA kernels for CNN convolution and GEMM and optimized image filtering and pooling pipelines.
Designed CTA-level schedulers that improved IPC and reduced L1/L2 cache miss rates.
Built a many-thread-aware prefetcher and CUDA micro-benchmark for validation.

projects.name: 32-bit MIPS Processor Design
projects.summary: Computer architecture project using Verilog, Design Compiler, and Mentor Graphics.
highlights: Architected and synthesized a 5-stage pipelined MIPS processor with registers and an arithmetic-logic unit.
Optimized 0.13 um ALU performance using a carry-lookahead adder, output selection, and logic reconfiguration.

projects.name: Memory-Augmented Transformer for Sequence Modeling
projects.summary: AI project using PyTorch and CUDA.
highlights: Developed autoregressive image generation on MNIST and language modeling on WikiText with memory optimization.
Explored memory-state processing and integration in memory-augmented Transformers for longer sequence processing.
